reasoning: I will provide a concise, factual summary of the article focusing on the key facts: the defendants, charges, the victim’s details, the sequence of events, the Facebook Live video, bail decision, and the fundraiser.
output: Four Chicago-area youths—Jordan Hill, Brittany Covington, and Tesfaye Cooper, all 18, and Tanishia Covington, 24—have been charged with hate crimes and aggravated kidnapping and battery, among other counts, in an assault on an 18-year-old white man with schizophrenia and attention deficit disorder. Prosecutors say the beating began in a stolen van and continued at a house, where the victim was forced to drink toilet water, kiss the floor, and was gagged and bound; a Facebook Live video captured derogatory remarks about whites and Donald Trump. Bail was denied. A fundraiser for the victim has raised about $51,000. The victim was dropped at a McDonald’s on December 31 and found January 3. Each suspect faces two hate crime counts—one for the victim’s race and one for his disabilities.
